nodes.name: Main Library Building Ground Floor
nodes.description: Serves as the primary transitional hub that leads from casual gathering and breakout spaces to focused quiet and deep-quiet study zones at the heart of the building.

nodes.name: Main Library Building First Floor
nodes.description: Housing the renowned Special Collections and serving as the repository for unique Hong Kong materials, rare books, and microforms essential for advanced research.

nodes.name: Main Library Building Second Floor
nodes.description: Housing the Ingenium and the Learning Hub, and serving as the primary digital innovation space that connects the main entrance to zones for creative collaboration, visualization, and production

nodes.name: Main Building
nodes.description: This 1912 Edwardian Baroque-style declared monument anchors the Main Campus with its iconic clock tower, red-brick façade, and Loke Yew Hall, originally housing the entire university and now serving as a blended space for teaching, major events, and central administration.

nodes.name: Hui Oi Chow Science Building
nodes.description: The connecting node bridging the Main Campus to the west, this six-story building opened in 1981 anchors scientific research by housing the Departments of Chemistry, Physics, and Earth Sciences, as well as the Tam Wing Fan Innovation Wing

nodes.name: Graduate House
nodes.description: The verdant residential anchor on the southwest slope, designed by Rocco Yim, merging student living, an amenity centre, and the Wang Gungwu Lecture Hall into the lush hillside while connecting the campus to the city below

nodes.name: Run Run Shaw Building Ground Floor
nodes.description: This floor is also home to the Tam Wing Fan Innovation Wing Two, a state-of-the-art research facility under the Faculty of Engineering

nodes.name: Run Run Shaw Building First Floor
nodes.description: The operational core of the Main Campus' Central cluster, historically housing the IT Services computer centre and now a functional floor within the Shaw Buildings complex above the lively podium and Innovation Wing below.

nodes.name: Knowles Building
nodes.description: The architectural soul of the Main Campus, this 1973 modernist landmark houses the entire Faculty of Architecture within its uniquely disorienting, flexible floor plates, wrapped in a distinctive brise-soleil façade and linked to the Main Library by a mid-level bridge

nodes.name: K.K. Leung Building Ground Floor
nodes.description: The bustling academic gateway of the K.K. Leung Building, strategically located near the main entrance levels to handle heavy student traffic, housing centrally timetabled lecture rooms.

nodes.name: K.K. Leung Building Lower Ground Floor
nodes.description: The instructional core and primary teaching zone of the K.K. Leung Building, located just below the main entrance concourse, housing centrally timetabled lecture rooms and seminar spaces to serve the heavy flow of student traffic

nodes.name: Meng Wah Complex Lower Ground Floor
nodes.description: The complex is a key part of campus life, housing the Faculty of Education, CEDARS, MWT1 lecture room

nodes.name: Meng Wah Complex Ground Floor
nodes.description: The complex is a key part of campus life, housing the Faculty of Education, CEDARS, MWT2 and MWT3 lecture room

nodes.name: Chong Yuet Ming Amenities Centre Third Floor
nodes.description: Linking the Chong Yuet Ming Amenities Centre to the University Street.

nodes.name: Chong Yuet Ming Amenities Centre Forth Floor
nodes.description: Linking Chong Yuet Ming Amenities Centre with Meng Wah Complex.

nodes.name: Chong Yuet Ming Physics Building Lower Ground Floor
nodes.description: The dedicated teaching and seminar hub of the Chong Yuet Ming Physics Building, located on LG1/F and featuring a cluster of centrally timetabled lecture theatres.

nodes.name: University Street Run Run Shaw Point
nodes.description: Connects Main Library, Run Run Shaw Building, Hui Oi Chow Science Building and Knowles Building to the University Street

nodes.name: SU Restaurant
nodes.description: The affordable communal dining hub of the Main Campus, commonly known as SU Restaurant or Union Restaurant.

nodes.name: Haking Wong Building Elevator Platform
nodes.description: Connects the HKU MTR Station Exit A2 to the rest of the Campus Levels.

nodes.name: Main Library Open Space
nodes.description: Passway between the Main Library, Knowles Building and K.K. Leung Building

nodes.name: HKU MTR Station Exit A2
nodes.description: Primary MTR arrival node for the Main Campus, reached via the lift-only Exit A2 connection on Pokfulam Road.

nodes.name: Haking Wong Building
nodes.description: Base-of-campus engineering and computer-science hub directly linked to the University Street covered walkway and the MTR side.

nodes.name: Composite Building
nodes.description: Upper University Street node between Haking Wong and the Centennial learning-spaces cluster.

nodes.name: Chow Yei Ching Building
nodes.description: Centennial-side podium building on the Pokfulam Road edge, commonly used as a transition node between Haking Wong and the Centennial cluster.

nodes.name: The Jockey Club Tower
nodes.description: The northernmost tower of the Centennial cluster, serving as the primary gateway from the University Street to the Faculty of Social Sciences.

nodes.name: Cheng Yu Tung Tower
nodes.description: Major Centennial Campus tower node connected to the podium system and close to the Jockey Club Tower vertical circulation core.

nodes.name: Chi Wah Learning Commons
nodes.description: Major Centennial learning hub connected to nearby lecture facilities and the upper covered walkway system.

nodes.name: Run Run Shaw Tower
nodes.description: The central anchor of the Centennial cluster, housing the Faculty of Arts and serving as the primary academic hub connecting the podium to the Grand Hall.

nodes.name: HKU MTR Station Exit C1
nodes.description: Primary MTR arrival node for the Centennial Campus, reached via the lift-only Exit C1 connection on Pokfulam Road.
